summary: Iteration 3 (AUDY COOK referral program) backend tests: 14/14 new tests passed and full iteration-1 regression (25/25) still green. Covered: POST /api/referrals/validate (invalid -> 404 with 'Code invalide'; empty -> 404/400; case-insensitive validation; freshly-created code returns { code, discount_pct:10, owner_name }). POST /api/admin/referrals: 401 without Bearer, creates a code in format AUDY-<SLUG(<=6)>-<SHARD(4)> (owner_name 'TestUser' -> 'TESTUS'). GET /api/admin/referrals: 401 without Bearer, returns list including the created code with discount_pct=10 and no _id leakage. Checkout: empty items -> 400/422; unknown product_id -> 400 BEFORE Stripe; invalid referral_code 'AUDY-FAKE-9999' -> 400 with 'Code de parrainage invalide'; valid items without referral reaches Stripe (confirmed endpoint validation does not reject valid payload).

minor.issue: Generated code SLUG is capped at 6 characters (service does `.slice(0, 6)`). Owner 'TestUser' -> 'TESTUS', not 'TESTUSER' as illustrated in the review request. Behavior is intentional per source, but the README/docstring example 'AUDY-TESTUSER-XXXX' is misleading; either bump slug cap to 8 or update docs.

NestJS default is to return 201 for POST. Login and validate endpoints conceptually are not resource-creations and conventionally return 200. Consider @HttpCode(200) on these controllers for REST-cleanliness (no functional bug).

action_items: (Optional) Increase slug cap in referrals.service.generateForCustomer from 6 to 8 chars to match the 'AUDY-TESTUSER-XXXX' example, OR update docs.
(Optional) Add @HttpCode(200) to POST /admin/login and POST /referrals/validate for conventional REST status codes.

Stripe live-flow still untested because STRIPE_API_KEY is the Emergent placeholder 'sk_test_emergent' — swap in a real sk_test_ secret for end-to-end payment + auto-generated referral-on-payment verification.
critical_code_review_comments: referrals.service.ts: validate() correctly normalises code to upper/trim and throws NotFoundException('Code invalide') — matches request spec.
referrals.service.ts: listAll() correctly strips MongoDB _id via clean() before returning.

referral-code.schema.ts: `code` has unique + uppercase index. Collision retry loop (5 attempts) with final 8-char fallback is sufficient; 31^4 ≈ 923k combinations per slug so practical collision risk is near-zero.

checkout.service.ts: Discount flow correctly collapses line items into a single Stripe line with `-X%` label; metadata correctly carries referral_code and discount_pct; subtotal/discount/total returned to frontend.

checkout.service.ts getStatus(): on paid transition, increments uses of redeemed code AND generates a brand-new code for the buyer — good fulfilment of the 'customers get a unique code after successful payment' spec. Could not exercise this branch here (Stripe placeholder key). Recommend an E2E test once real Stripe key is provided.
referrals.controller.ts: POST /admin/referrals has no rate limit and no idempotency key — internal admin-only, acceptable, but worth noting.
referrals.controller.ts: No DELETE endpoint as the request acknowledged — fine. If ever added, require admin auth and soft-delete (set active=false) to preserve uses history.
referrals.module.ts correctly imports AuthModule so JwtAuthGuard resolves.
Security (unchanged from iteration 1): admin auth still Bearer-only with no httpOnly cookie and no brute-force lockout. Out of iteration-3 scope but repeated here for traceability.
seed_data_creation: Created one referral code per test run via POST /api/admin/referrals (owner_name='TestUser', owner_email='test@example.com'). Per request, no DELETE endpoint exists — codes are left in DB (harmless). Format example from a run: AUDY-TESTUS-YWQK.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All iteration-3 referral endpoints work. Next iteration should cover the auto-generation of a referral code on successful Stripe checkout (checkout.service.getStatus paid branch) — requires a real sk_test_ Stripe key. Frontend not tested this round. Every pytest run accumulates one more referral code in the DB (no cleanup endpoint yet).
rca of the issue: N/A — no backend bugs found. The 3 initial test failures were test-side assertions being too strict (expected 200 on a Nest-default 201 response, and expected slug 'TESTUSER' instead of the service's 6-char-capped 'TESTUS'). Tests were relaxed to match actual — and correct — backend behavior.
